description: Legacy Oracle 11g OLTP database, 18 years old, running on Sun SPARC hardware with custom kernel patches, 47 stored procedure packages, Oracle Forms UI layer, no containerization, team of 12 with no cloud skills, $180k/yr Oracle support contract, business-critical ERP core.
rationale: Custom SPARC hardware dependency and Oracle Forms lock-in make cloud migration infeasible without a full rewrite; $180k licensing is sunk cost not easily replaced; Retain pending a funded Refactor program.

description: Stateless Python Flask REST API, 3 years old, 12 microservice endpoints, Dockerized, CI/CD on GitHub Actions, PostgreSQL backend on RDS already, team of 6 cloud-native engineers, no proprietary dependencies, 99.5% uptime target, deployed on bare EC2.
rationale: Already containerized and cloud-adjacent; moving from bare EC2 to ECS Fargate or App Runner requires minimal code change — classic lift-tinker-shift. Not Refactor because no decomposition needed; not Rehost because managed compute is a clear win.

description: COBOL batch processing system on IBM z/OS mainframe, handles overnight payroll calculations for 80k employees, 35 years old, 1.2M lines of COBOL, tight coupling to VSAM file system, small team of 3 COBOL specialists nearing retirement, no documentation, business-critical.
rationale: VSAM coupling, COBOL expertise scarcity, and regulatory sensitivity of payroll make this a Retain until a funded Refactor program can rewrite in a modern language; forcing a lift-and-shift risks data integrity failures.

description: On-premises Salesforce-clone CRM built in 2014 on ASP.NET WebForms, 8 users, $120k/yr total cost of ownership, no integrations, team spends 60% of time on maintenance patches, identical functionality available in Salesforce Sales Cloud at $25/user/mo.
rationale: Commodity CRM functionality with a SaaS equivalent available at 80% lower TCO; small user base makes migration trivial; no competitive differentiation from custom code.

description: Internal file share server running Windows Server 2012 R2 on aging Dell PowerEdge hardware, 200 GB of documents, 30 users, no special compliance requirements, lease on physical server expires in 4 months, IT team wants to eliminate all on-prem servers.
rationale: Pure lift-and-shift to S3 or Azure Files; no application logic to refactor; time pressure from expiring hardware lease; Replatform is overkill for a file share.

description: Java 8 Spring Boot monolith, e-commerce platform, 6 years old, 280k lines of code, 40+ microservice-like modules but all deployed as one WAR, PostgreSQL, Redis cache, team of 18 engineers familiar with AWS, containerized locally but deployed on bare VMs, peak Black Friday traffic 50x normal.
rationale: Pseudo-monolith with clear service boundaries; experienced cloud team; extreme traffic variance demands auto-scaling; Refactor into proper microservices on EKS/ECS to handle Black Friday spikes cost-effectively.

description: Windows Server 2016 application server running a licensed third-party supply-chain management software (SAP EWM), vendor-managed updates, 50 users, SAP recently announced an equivalent SaaS SKU (SAP EWM Cloud) included in existing S/4HANA Cloud subscription.
rationale: Vendor-provided SaaS upgrade path included in existing contract; eliminates Windows Server licensing and maintenance overhead; classic Repurchase scenario.

description: Perl CGI web application for internal HR policy document retrieval, built in 2008, 3 active users per week, no updates in 7 years, functionality now duplicated in the company's SharePoint intranet, original developer left the company.
rationale: Duplicate functionality covered by SharePoint; negligible active usage; no developer to maintain it; retiring eliminates a security liability with zero business impact.

description: Node.js Express API gateway, 2 years old, stateless, JWT authentication, AWS Lambda-compatible code structure but deployed on EC2 t3.medium, 1200 req/s peak, team of 4, 100% unit test coverage, no persistent state, cost target is to cut infrastructure spend by 40%.
rationale: Lambda-compatible code structure means migration to API Gateway + Lambda is configuration, not rewriting; 40% cost target is achievable with serverless; minimal engineering effort required.

description: Oracle E-Business Suite 12.2 instance on bare metal, 15 years old, 800 custom RICE objects (Reports, Interfaces, Conversions, Extensions), heavily customized GL and AR modules, Oracle still supporting 12.2 until 2032, team of 5 Oracle specialists, $500k/yr support.
rationale: 800 custom RICE objects make cloud migration a multi-year, multi-million dollar program; Oracle's 12.2 support until 2032 removes urgency; Retain until business case for Refactor/Repurchase is funded.

description: Apache Kafka cluster on-premises, 3-broker setup, 120 topics, 15 TB/day throughput, 8 producer applications and 22 consumer microservices already running on Kubernetes on-prem, team actively evaluating cloud migration, no vendor lock-in, 95th percentile latency target 50ms.
rationale: Kafka on-prem to AWS MSK or Confluent Cloud is a well-trodden Replatform; consumer/producer apps on K8s can migrate to EKS with minimal change; managed broker eliminates ops overhead without re-architecting producers/consumers.

description: Legacy VB6 Windows desktop application for manufacturing floor scheduling, used by 8 operators, last updated 2011, no source code available (binary only), vendor out of business, Windows 10 compatibility shim required, equivalent SaaS (Fishbowl Manufacturing) costs $18k/yr.
rationale: No source code eliminates Refactor/Replatform options; binary-only with compatibility shim is a reliability risk; Fishbowl SaaS equivalent available; Repurchase is the only viable path.

description: Internal test result reporting tool built as a PHP 5.6 web app, used only by the QA team (6 people), generates PDF reports from a MySQL database, no external integrations, was built by a contractor in 2013, barely functional, team has been requesting Jira integration for 3 years.
rationale: PHP 5.6 is EOL; minimal usage; requested Jira integration signals the team wants to replace it anyway; native Jira reporting or Confluence covers the use case; Retire and point users to existing tooling.

description: High-frequency trading signal computation service, C++ on bare metal with kernel bypass networking (DPDK), sub-millisecond latency SLA, custom network cards, 14 years of proprietary algorithm IP baked into the binary, competitive advantage depends on latency, cloud latency is 10x worse than co-lo.
rationale: DPDK + kernel bypass = cloud VMs cannot match the latency profile; the competitive moat IS the custom hardware stack; Retain in co-location; cloud migration would destroy the business advantage.

description: Python 3.10 ML inference service, containerized, serving a scikit-learn fraud detection model, 50 req/s average, deployed on m5.xlarge EC2, model retrained weekly via SageMaker, team of 3 MLEs, autoscaling not configured, monthly costs $800.
rationale: Already containerized; move to ECS with autoscaling or SageMaker real-time endpoint; minimal code change; autoscaling resolves cost and capacity issues; not Refactor because no architectural change needed.

description: On-premises Microsoft Exchange 2010 email server, 450 mailboxes, 3TB of email data, hardware out of support, IT team of 2, Exchange 2010 EOL since October 2020, multiple unpatched CVEs, Microsoft 365 Business Basic available at $6/user/mo.
rationale: Exchange 2010 is EOL with active CVEs — a security liability; Microsoft 365 migration is the canonical upgrade path Microsoft designed it for; 450 seats at $6/mo is $32k/yr vs $200k+ for new Exchange infrastructure.

description: Real-time IoT telemetry ingestion pipeline built on Apache Flink on Kubernetes, 6 months old, processes 2M events/sec, cloud-native design with no proprietary dependencies, team of 5 senior engineers, AWS Kinesis Data Analytics already evaluated and rejected for cost at this scale.
rationale: Already cloud-native on K8s; team evaluated and rejected managed Kinesis due to cost — signals they want infrastructure control; Rehost to EKS preserves the architecture while eliminating on-prem hardware; not Replatform because managed service was rejected.

description: Custom employee scheduling web app built in 2019 on React + Node.js, 200 users, the company recently signed a contract with Workday HCM which includes Advanced Scheduling as a module, go-live of Workday is in 6 months.
rationale: Workday Advanced Scheduling will replace this exact functionality in 6 months; maintaining the custom app until cutover is the only sensible choice, then Retire — future state is already decided.

description: Ruby on Rails monolith for a SaaS product, 9 years old, 180k lines, serving 4,000 paying customers, Rails 5.2 (EOL), PostgreSQL, no caching layer, 8s average page load time on product pages, engineering team of 22, technical debt backlog has 600 items, competitors average 1.2s load time.
rationale: Customer-facing SaaS with 8s page load vs 1.2s competitor average is a churn risk; Rails 5.2 EOL adds security exposure; 22-person team has capacity; Refactor to Rails 7 + fragment caching + background job separation needed; not Repurchase because this IS the product.

description: Windows Server 2019 IIS web server hosting a static marketing website (20 HTML/CSS/JS files, no server-side logic, updated once per quarter), $400/mo in EC2 + EBS costs, marketing team wants to take ownership of content updates.
rationale: Static site with no server-side logic belongs on S3 + CloudFront or a CDN; eliminates Windows Server licensing and reduces cost to ~$5/mo; CloudFront gives marketing team easy deployment via S3 sync; trivial migration.

description: IBM AS/400 (iSeries) running RPG IV business logic for a regional bank's core banking system, 28 years old, processes $2B/day in transactions, 800k lines of RPG, regulatory requirement to have 99.999% uptime, no cloud provider has achieved OCC certification for core banking on public cloud in this jurisdiction.
rationale: Regulatory OCC certification gap makes public cloud legally impossible; $2B/day transaction volume makes any migration a multi-year program requiring regulatory approval; Retain until jurisdiction certifies cloud for core banking.

description: Docker Compose-based data pipeline (Python ETL scripts + PostgreSQL + Redis), 1.5 years old, runs on a single on-prem server, 4 GB/day data processed, team of 2 data engineers, no redundancy, server failure caused 6-hour outage last month.
rationale: Docker Compose → ECS or Fargate with managed RDS Aurora and ElastiCache Redis; single point of failure drives urgency; minimal code changes required; adds HA and managed backups; Rehost to EC2 would just move the SPOF to cloud.

description: On-premises Apache Hadoop cluster (20 nodes) for batch analytics, 7 years old, 80% of jobs now run on Databricks on AWS (migrated over the past 2 years), remaining 20% are legacy Pig/Hive jobs nobody has time to rewrite, cluster costs $15k/mo to operate.
rationale: 80% already migrated; remaining 20% are unmaintained legacy jobs; $15k/mo operating cost for 20% workload is indefensible; Retire the cluster and either rewrite the 20% on Databricks or determine they are no longer needed.
